info.title: Element Aggregator API for B2B
info.description: The Element Aggregator acts as a bridge between fiat and blockchain smart contracts. It handles payment processing, event listening, and order management, ensuring seamless coordination between Web2 and Web3 ecosystems.

post.description:
Register a new user account.

- **email**: Must be a valid email address
- **password**: Must be at least 8 characters long, contain at least one number,
  one uppercase and one lowercase letter
- **role**: Optional, defaults to 'user'

Verify a user's email address using the verification code.

- **email**: User's email address
- **verification_code**: The verification code sent to the user's email

schema.title: Response Verify Email Auth Verify Email Post

post.description:
Request a password reset code to be sent to the user's email.

- **email**: User's email address

schema.title: Response Request Password Reset Auth Password Reset Request Post

post.description:
Reset user's password using the reset code.

- **email**: User's email address
- **reset_code**: The reset code sent to the user's email
- **new_password**: The new password to set

schema.title: Response Reset Password Auth Password Reset Confirm Post

post.description:
Get a new access token using a refresh token.

- **refresh_token**: A valid refresh token

Change the authenticated user's password.

- **current_password**: Current password
- **new_password**: New password (must meet complexity requirements)

schema.title: Response Change Password Auth Password Change Post

post.description:
Resend email verification code to user's email address.

- **email**: User's email address

 Note: Always returns the same success message regardless of whether 
the email exists, is already verified, or a new code was sent to prevent 
user enumeration attacks.

Returns:
{
  "status": "success",
  "message": "If an account exists, a verification email has been sent.",
  "data": null
}

schema.title: Response Resend Verification Auth Resend Verification Post

get.description:
Get the currently authenticated user's information.

Requires a valid access token in the Authorization header.

post.description:
Request an OTP code for email-based authentication (dapp).

- If user exists: generates new OTP and sends email
- If user does not exist: creates user with placeholder password, generates OTP, sends email

Always returns the same success message to prevent email enumeration.

- **email**: User's email address

200.description: OTP request accepted (email sent if account exists or was created)

example.message: If an account exists or was created, a verification code has been sent to your email.

400.description: Bad request (invalid email format or email verification failed)

value.message: Email address is from a disposable email provider and cannot be used

post.summary: Verify OTP and get JWT tokens
post.description:
Verify OTP code and return JWT tokens (auto "login").

- Verifies the 6-digit code sent to email
- Activates user account if new
- Returns access_token, refresh_token, and user data

- **email**: User's email address
- **verification_code**: 6-digit verification code

200.description: OTP verified successfully, tokens and user data returned

400.description: Bad request (invalid or expired verification code)

post.summary: Connect a wallet to authenticated user
post.description:
Connect a wallet address to the authenticated user.

- Requires JWT authentication
- Enforces one-wallet-per-user: returns 409 if wallet already linked to another account
- If wallet already linked to same user, returns existing wallet
- Otherwise, creates new wallet entry

- **address**: Wallet address (e.g., 0x...)

200.description: Wallet already linked to this user (returns existing wallet)

409.description: Conflict (wallet already linked to another user)

example.message: This wallet is already linked to another account.

Get all wallets linked to the authenticated user.

Requires a valid access token in the Authorization header.

Returns a list of all wallets associated with the current user.

post.description:
Minimal webhook verifier.
Steps:
  1) Read raw request body (bytes)
  2) Parse `X-Webhook-Signature`: "t=<unix_ts>,v1=<base64sig>"
  3) Enforce a small timestamp window (default 5 minutes)
  4) Recompute signature and compare in constant time
  5) If valid, parse JSON and return 200

Handles `OrderCreated` events emitted by the ElementPay smart contract coming from the listener.

This endpoint is called by a Web3 listener whenever an `OrderCreated`
event is detected on-chain. The listener transforms the event arguments into a structured
payload and sends a POST request to this route with cryptographic signature verification.

Authentication:
- Uses HMAC-SHA256 signature verification via verify_listener_signature dependency
- Timestamp-based replay protection (configurable skew tolerance)
- Shared secret authentication between listener and API

Workflow:
1. **Signature Verification**: Validates request authenticity using HMAC signatures
2. **Event Processing**: Converts the payload (including hex strings) into internal structures
3. **Order Creation**: Passes the parsed event to `process_order_created()` for handling:
    - Decrypts messageHash to extract transaction details (phone, amount, etc)
    - Creates the order in the database with blockchain transaction reference
    - Based on the `orderType`:
        - **OnRamp**: Initiates an M-PESA STK push (C2B) for user payment
        - **OffRamp**: Initiates a B2C or B2B disbursement via Safaricom API
4. **Response**: Returns `{"status": "ok"}` on success

Required Headers (handled by verify_listener_signature):
- X-EP-Timestamp: Unix timestamp of request creation
- X-EP-Signature: HMAC signature in format "v1={hex_digest}"
- Content-Type: application/json

Error Responses:
- **401 Unauthorized**: Invalid or missing signature, stale timestamp
- **400 Bad Request**: Invalid phone number, malformed payload
- **502 Bad Gateway**: STK push failed, missing Safaricom/payment response  
- **500 Internal Server Error**: Unhandled internal errors

Example Request from Listener:
```
POST /order-created
Headers:
  Content-Type: application/json
  X-EP-Timestamp: 1692547200
  X-EP-Signature: v1=a4b8c2d3e4f5...
Body:
  {
    "orderId": "0x1234...",
    "requester": "0xabcd...",
    "token": "0x5678...",
    "amount": "1000000",
    "messageHash": "0xdef0...",
    "orderType": 1,
    "transactionHash": "0x9abc..."
  }
```

Handles `OrderSettled` events from the ElementPay smart contract.

This endpoint is called by the blockchain listener when an order's crypto settlement
is confirmed on-chain. It updates the order status to SETTLED and records the
settlement transaction hash for audit purposes.

Authentication:
- HMAC-SHA256 signature verification via verify_listener_signature dependency
- Timestamp-based replay protection with shared secret authentication

Workflow:
1. Validates orderId is present in payload
2. Normalizes order ID (removes 0x prefix, converts to lowercase)
3. Fetches order from database
4. Checks if order is already settled (idempotent operation)
5. Updates order status to SETTLED with settlement transaction hash

Required Headers:
- X-EP-Timestamp: Unix timestamp of request creation  
- X-EP-Signature: HMAC signature in "v1={hex_digest}" format

Payload Format:
- orderId: Hex string identifier from blockchain event
- transactionHash: Settlement transaction hash on blockchain

Returns:
- 200: Order successfully updated or already settled
- 400: Missing orderId in payload
- 401: Invalid signature or stale timestamp
- 404: Order not found in database

Security Notes:
- All requests cryptographically authenticated from trusted listener
- Idempotent operation prevents duplicate settlement processing
- Transaction hash provides blockchain audit trail

Handles `OrderRefunded` events from the ElementPay smart contract.

This endpoint is called by the blockchain listener when an order is refunded
on-chain (typically due to timeout, cancellation, or failed processing).
Updates the order status to REFUNDED and records the refund transaction.

Authentication:
- HMAC-SHA256 signature verification via verify_listener_signature dependency  
- Timestamp-based replay protection with shared secret authentication

Workflow:
1. Validates orderId is present in payload
2. Normalizes order ID (removes 0x prefix, converts to lowercase)
3. Fetches order from database
4. Checks if order is already refunded (idempotent operation)
5. Updates order status to REFUNDED with refund transaction hash

Required Headers:
- X-EP-Timestamp: Unix timestamp of request creation
- X-EP-Signature: HMAC signature in "v1={hex_digest}" format

Payload Format:
- orderId: Hex string identifier from blockchain event
- transactionHash: Refund transaction hash on blockchain

Returns:
- 200: Order successfully marked as refunded or already refunded
- 400: Missing orderId in payload  
- 401: Invalid signature or stale timestamp
- 404: Order not found in database

Use Cases:
- Order timeout (user didn't complete payment in time)
- Manual cancellation by user or admin
- System failure during processing requiring rollback
- Failed M-PESA transactions that need blockchain refund

Security Notes:
- All requests cryptographically authenticated from trusted listener
- Idempotent operation prevents duplicate refund processing  
- Transaction hash provides blockchain audit trail for refunds

Handles C2B Validation callbacks from M-Pesa (Safaricom).

This endpoint is SPECIFICALLY for M-Pesa Paybill validation. Other PSPs (Cellulant, etc.)
may have their own validation processes and endpoints.

This endpoint is called by M-Pesa BEFORE completing a transaction when External Validation
is enabled. It validates that the payment should be accepted or rejected.

Flow:
1. M-Pesa sends validation request with payment details (BillRefNumber, amount, etc.)
2. We validate the client_id (BillRefNumber) exists in our system
3. We check user is active and has wallet_address configured
4. We check sufficient gas and token balance for order processing
5. We respond with ResultCode "0" to accept, or error code to reject
6. M-Pesa processes transaction based on our response

IMPORTANT: Response must be sent within ~8 seconds or M-Pesa will use default action.

Safaricom M-Pesa Error Codes (as per official documentation):
    - ResultCode "0" + ResultDesc "Accepted" = Accept payment
    - ResultCode "C2B00011" + ResultDesc "Invalid MSISDN" = Reject (invalid phone number)
    - ResultCode "C2B00012" + ResultDesc "Invalid Account Number" = Reject (client_id not found)
    - ResultCode "C2B00013" + ResultDesc "Invalid Amount" = Reject (invalid amount)
    - ResultCode "C2B00014" + ResultDesc "Invalid KYC Details" = Reject (user inactive/no wallet)
    - ResultCode "C2B00015" + ResultDesc "Invalid Short code" = Reject (invalid Paybill/Till number)
    - ResultCode "C2B00016" + ResultDesc "Other Error" = Reject (gas/token balance issues, etc.)

Returns:
    JSON response with ResultCode and ResultDesc matching Safaricom format

Handles C2B Confirmation callbacks from M-Pesa.

This is triggered after a manual Paybill payment or STK push **if external validation is off**.

It is used to confirm that a payment has been completed, and the system should record it.

Flow:
1. If `BillRefNumber` starts with "EPay-", route to STK handler (existing STK push flow)
2. Otherwise, treat as manual Paybill payment:
   - Look up user by client_id (BillRefNumber)
   - Create order on-chain and in DB
   - Send merchant SMS notification

Returns a 200 OK JSON response to acknowledge.

Handles M-Pesa STK Push (C2B) result callbacks.

Triggered automatically by M-Pesa after a STK Push request is accepted or rejected.

This typically contains structured JSON under `Body.stkCallback`.

Calls `handle_failure_callback()` which handles both success and failure cases.

Returns 200 OK JSON to acknowledge.

🔁 Handles M-PESA B2C Queue Timeout Callback

This endpoint is hit by Safaricom's API if a B2C payment request times out
while in their internal queue. This is not a definitive failure, just a notification
that the request could not be processed immediately.

It attempts to locate the related Order using `OriginatorConversationID` and marks
it as FAILED with `failure_reason='Timeout'`. If no order is found, logs a warning.

Docs: https://developer.safaricom.co.ke/APIs/M-PESA-B2C

Payload Example:
{
    "Result": {
        "ResultType": 0,
        "ResultCode": 1,
        "ResultDesc": "Queue timeout",
        "OriginatorConversationID": "FILE123",
        ...
    }
}

Returns:
    dict: A simple status acknowledgment.

schema.title: Response Handle B2C Timeout Payments B2C Timeout Post
Response Handle B2C Result Payments B2C Result Post

post.description:
Handles the B2B timeout callback from Safaricom.

This endpoint is triggered when M-PESA is unable to complete a B2B transaction
within the specified timeframe. The system attempts to mark the associated order
as failed using the `OriginatorConversationID`.

Returns:
    dict: JSON acknowledgment to Safaricom

schema.title: Response Handle B2B Timeout Payments B2B Businessbuygoods Queue Post

post.description:
Handles the B2B result callback from Safaricom.

This endpoint processes the final status of a B2B transaction (success or failure).
It identifies the order using the `OriginatorConversationID`, then either:
- Marks it as SETTLED on success, or
- Marks it as FAILED and optionally triggers a refund on failure.

Returns:
    dict: JSON acknowledgment to Safaricom

schema.title: Response Handle B2B Result Payments B2B Businessbuygoods Result Post

post.description:
Handles Cellulant checkout/collection result callbacks (OnRamp - customer pays merchant).

This endpoint is called by Cellulant after a checkout-charge (STK Push) is processed.
It receives the final status of the collection and updates the order accordingly.

Cellulant Checkout Status Codes:
- 183: Payment successful
- 180: Payment failed
- 178: Payment pending

Payload Example:
{
    "checkout_request_id": "12345",
    "merchant_transaction_id": "EPay-abc123",
    "status_code": 183,
    "status_description": "Payment successful",
    "amount": 100.00,
    "currency_code": "KES",
    "msisdn": "254712345678",
    "account_number": "TinggAcc01",
    "payment_date": "2025-10-22 12:00:00",
    ...
}

Returns:
    dict: Acknowledgment to Cellulant

schema.title: Response Cellulant Checkout Callback Payments Cellulant Checkout Callback Post

post.description:
Handles Cellulant BEEP payout result callbacks.

This endpoint is called by Cellulant after a B2C or B2B payout is processed.
It receives the final status of the transaction and updates the order accordingly.

Cellulant Status Codes:
- 183: Payment accepted (Success)
- 180: Payment rejected (Failed)
- 178: Pending transaction
- 148: Transaction delivered to 3rd party
- 217: Payment manually accepted
- 216: Payment manually rejected

Payload Example:
{
    "beepTransactionID": "387566457040408576",
    "payerTransactionID": "FILE-abc123",
    "statusCode": 183,
    "statusDescription": "Payment accepted",
    "MSISDN": "254712345678",
    "receiptNumber": "RCT123",
    ...
}

Returns:
    dict: Acknowledgment to Cellulant

schema.title: Response Cellulant Payout Callback Payments Cellulant Payout Callback Post

post.description:
Handles C2B callback results from SasaPay.

This endpoint receives the final transaction result for both:
- STK Push payments (OnRamp)
- Manual Paybill payments (C2B)

Callback Format:
{
    "MerchantRequestID": "12345678",
    "CheckoutRequestID": "31336ba8-d057-4f2b-9a15-b5aa0af7d20f",
    "PaymentRequestID": "PR45429827",
    "ResultCode": "0",  # "0" = success
    "ResultDesc": "Transaction processed successfully.",
    "SourceChannel": "M-PESA",
    "TransAmount": "1.000000",
    "RequestedAmount": "1.00",
    "Paid": true,
    "BillRefNumber": "12345678",  # Account reference
    "TransactionDate": "20251218134718",
    "CustomerMobile": "254712345678",
    "TransactionCode": "SPEJ47VZTWXC49G",
    "ThirdPartyTransID": "TLINT1E2UB"
}

Returns:
    200 OK JSON response to acknowledge receipt

get.description:
DEV/TESTING: Manually check Cellulant payment status.

Use this endpoint to manually query the status of a Cellulant payment.
Useful for testing and debugging when callbacks aren't being received.

NOTE: For Kenya (KE), payerTransactionID (file_id) is REQUIRED.

Args:
    file_id: Your payerTransactionID (e.g., "EPay-1d50761a59") - REQUIRED
    beep_tx_id: Cellulant's beepTransactionID (optional, for verification)
    
Example:
    GET /dev/cellulant/status/EPay-1d50761a59
    GET /dev/cellulant/status/EPay-1d50761a59?beep_tx_id=765227391332126720

Returns:
    Payment status from Cellulant BEEP API

🔧 DEV/TESTING: Check Cellulant status for a specific order.

Looks up the order and queries its Cellulant payment status.

Example:
    GET /dev/cellulant/check-order/0fe0c26e2f29375a0b62cc6a1a5c2b2e75deb50fbb1ad4425beb7ca33f9b6d62

Returns:
    Order info and current Cellulant payment status

post.description:
DEV/TESTING: Validate if a phone number/account is valid for Cellulant payments.

Uses Cellulant's BEEP.validateAccount API to check if an account is valid
before initiating a payment.

Useful for:
- Frontend validation before order creation
- Manual checks during testing
- Pre-validation in admin panel

Example:
    POST /dev/cellulant/validate-account
    Body: {"phone_number": "254712345678"}

Returns:
{
    "valid": true,                       // Whether account is valid
    "customer_name": "John Doe",         // Account holder name
    "account_number": "254712345678",    // Validated account number
    "active": true,                      // Whether account is active
    "status_code": 307,                  // Cellulant status code
    "message": "Account number is valid" // Status description
}

Status Codes (from Cellulant):
    - 307: Account is valid 
    - 306: Invalid account number 
    - 301: Validation not available for this service
    - 167: Invalid service/not configured

delete.description:
Revoke (soft-delete) a specific API key in the current environment.

- Marks `revoked=True` on the record
- Only revokes keys that belong to the current user and environment
- Returns `{"revoked_count": 1, "key_id": 123, "environment": "sandbox"}` on success
- 404 if the key doesn't exist, is already revoked, or belongs to different environment

Webhook secret cannot be empty or just whitespace

get.description:
Public endpoint — no API key required.

Returns:
  - rate: KES per token **after markup**
  - token_amount: tokens user would receive (OnRamp) / must send (OffRamp)
  - fiat_paid: echo of amount_fiat
  - symbol/decimals: metadata (no chain implied)

400.description: Bad request (invalid order_type or unsupported token, etc.)

post.summary: Calculate order quote with optional balance check
post.description:
Calculate the required token amount for an order and optionally check balance/allowance.

This endpoint helps clients determine:
- How much they need to approve before creating an order
- Whether they have sufficient balance/allowance (if wallet_address provided)

Returns:
    - required_token_amount: Amount in standard units (readable)
    - required_token_amount_raw: Amount in smallest units (for approval)
    - fee_amount: Fee charged in fiat
    - effective_rate: Rate used for conversion
    - If wallet_address provided for OffRamp: current balance, allowance, and sufficiency flags

get.summary: Daily transaction counts by order type
get.description:
Retrieve daily transaction counts for OnRamp and OffRamp orders.

This endpoint aggregates transaction data by day and order type, providing counts for both OnRamp (incoming) and OffRamp (outgoing) transactions. The data is intended for line graph visualizations of transaction trends over time.

Query Parameters:
- start_date (optional, ISO 8601): Start of the date range. Defaults to 7 days before end_date if not provided.
- end_date (optional, ISO 8601): End of the date range. Defaults to today if not provided.
- status (optional, List[OrderStatus]): Filter transactions by status.
    - If omitted, **all orders are included regardless of status**.
    - To include only specific business-relevant states, pass a list of statuses (e.g., completed, settled).

- Returns a list of daily counts, with each day containing separate counts for OnRamp and OffRamp orders.
- Days with no transactions will still be included with counts set to 0.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by order status. Can provide multiple status values
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by order status. Can provide multiple status values

value.message: Invalid date format: '2024-01-32'. Dates must be valid ISO 8601 format.
start_date cannot be greater than end_date
Invalid status value: 'invalid_status'. Must be one of: pending, processing, completed, failed, cancelled, settled.
End date cannot be in the future

get.description:
Retrieve per-user transaction metrics for admin analytics.

Metrics per user:
- userId: User ID
- email: User email
- totalTransactions: Total number of transactions (all statuses)
- totalVolume: Sum of transaction amounts in the specified currency
- totalRevenue: Revenue after fees and costs (only from SETTLED/REFUNDED orders)

Note: totalTransactions counts ALL orders (including PENDING, FAILED, etc.),
while totalRevenue only includes earnings from SETTLED and REFUNDED orders.
Users with only pending/failed orders will show transactions but $0 revenue.

Filters:
- start_date / end_date: Date range (defaults to last 30 days)
- email: Email prefix match (case-insensitive, better performance than full partial match)
- currency: Currency code (default: KES)

Pagination:
- limit: Maximum number of users to return
- offset: Number of users to skip

Access: Admin only.

Returns: JSON with items (list of per-user metrics), pagination metadata, and applied filters.

Set the preferred PSP for a capability (legacy: ``psp_state_manager``).

For production routing, prefer ``psp_configurations`` priority and rules in
MySQL (see ``DB_ROUTING_OPERATIONS.md``). This endpoint remains for legacy
fallback that still reads preferred PSP from JSON state.

Args:

    request: Preferred PSP request

Returns:

Examples:
    - Set Cellulant for B2C payouts: POST /admin/psp/capabilities/b2c_payout/preferred-psp {"psp_name": "cellulant"}
    - Switch to M-Pesa: POST /admin/psp/capabilities/b2c_payout/preferred-psp {"psp_name": "mpesa"}
    - Use priority-based: POST /admin/psp/capabilities/b2c_payout/preferred-psp {"psp_name": null}

Idempotent: only **missing** rows are inserted; existing rows for the same
rail + name + environment are left unchanged. Uses the same DB connection
pool as the app (``DATABASE_URL`` / aiomysql), not ``SYNC_DATABASE_URL``.

Safe to call from Docker after migrations when shell access is awkward.

get.description:
Get list of supported banks for bank transfers.

Public endpoint - no authentication required.
Used by frontends to show bank selection dropdowns.

Args:
    country: Country code (default: "KE" for Kenya)
    search: Optional search query to filter banks
    
Returns:
    List of supported banks with codes
    
Examples:
    - GET /api/v1/banks - Get all Kenya banks (35 banks)
    - GET /api/v1/banks?search=equity - Search for Equity Bank
    - GET /api/v1/banks?search=0068 - Search by bank code
    - GET /api/v1/banks?search=commercial - Find commercial banks

schema.title: Response Get Supported Banks Banks Get

get.description:
Get details of a specific bank by code.

Public endpoint - no authentication required.
Useful for validating bank codes and showing bank names.

Args:
    bank_code: Bank code (e.g., "0001" for KCB, "0068" for Equity)
    
Returns:
    Bank details including name, short name, and type
    
Examples:
    - GET /api/v1/banks/0001 - Get KCB details
    - GET /api/v1/banks/68 - Get Equity Bank (auto-normalizes to 0068)

post.description:
Validate a bank code.

Public endpoint - useful for frontend validation before submitting transactions.

Args:
    request: Bank validation request with code and country
    
Returns:
    Validation result with bank details if valid
    
Example:
    POST /api/v1/banks/validate
    {
      "bank_code": "0068",
      "country": "KE"
    }
    
    Response:
    {
      "valid": true,
      "bank_code": "0068",
      "bank": {
        "name": "Equity Bank",
        "short_name": "Equity Bank",
        "code": "0068",
        "bank_type": "Commercial Bank",
        "country": "KE"
      }
    }

Initiates KYC verification by generating a SmileID Smile Link.
    
    **Cost Optimization:**
    - If user is already KYC verified, returns immediately without creating a new link
    - If user has an existing active (non-expired) link, returns that link
    - Only creates a new SmileID link if necessary
    
    The returned link should be opened by the user in a browser to complete verification.

200.description: KYC link returned (existing link reused or user already verified)

value.message: User has already completed KYC verification

data.message: User has already completed KYC verification

example.message: Failed to create KYC verification link. Please try again later.

get.description: Get the current KYC verification status for the authenticated user.

post.description:
Receives KYC verification results from SmileID.

    This endpoint is called by SmileID when a user completes or fails KYC verification.
    The callback includes the verification result code and user reference information.

    **Idempotency:**
    - Duplicate callbacks for already-processed KYC links are ignored
    - Always returns 200 OK for valid/successfully processed requests regardless of internal outcome

link_not_found.summary: KYC link not found for ref_id

post.description:
Receives ``EventType: Customer`` payloads after hosted onboarding status changes.

Verifies ``Webhook-Signature`` (unless ``NOAH_WEBHOOK_SKIP_VERIFY``), then updates
``psp_customer_links`` for **organization** + rail ``noah`` only. Returns **200** on
success or benign ignore so Noah does not retry unnecessarily.

get.summary: Get enrollment link by subject + rail (any PSP)
get.description:
Return the ``psp_customer_links`` row for ``(subject_type, subject_id, rail_key)``.

``subject_type`` must be ``user`` or ``organization`` (case-insensitive). Accepted
aliases: ``organisation`` → ``organization``; ``individual``, ``person``,
``natural_person`` → ``user``.

Works for any ``rail_key`` (Noah, a future Yellow Card key, etc.). The default
``rail_key=noah`` is historical convenience; multi-PSP callers should pass the rail
explicitly. **404** if no row exists. **422** if ``subject_type`` is unknown.
Does not call external PSP APIs — database only.

post.summary: [Noah] Reliance: PUT customer + persist enrollment link
post.description:
**Noah-only** route (first PSP in this module): Reliance synchronous customer create/update.

Ensures a ``psp_customer_links`` row, calls Noah ``PUT /v1/customers/{CustomerID}`` via
:class:`~app.services.psp.noah.enrollment.NoahEnrollmentService`, then persists status
via :class:`~app.repositories.psp_customer_link_repository.PSPCustomerLinkRepository`.
Other PSPs should follow the same pattern in new handlers, not inside this function.

**Body rules**

- ``subject_type`` ``user``: ``user_id`` optional — set it when a ``users`` row exists
  (validated if present); omit for externally-keyed individuals (use a stable ``subject_id``).
- ``organization``: ``user_id`` must be null.

**Responses**

- **200** with link + Noah HTTP metadata on success.
- **422** for invalid body or missing required Noah fields (e.g. ``Type``).
- **502** when Noah returns an error (upstream status and body echoed in ``detail``).
  Common: **401** ``signature not provided`` — set ``NOAH_REQUEST_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY``
  (see module docstring).
- **503** when Noah env is not configured, internal enroll key missing, or **signing
  PEM is set but JWT build fails** (wrong PEM / algorithm vs key curve).

Full path (with default app prefix): ``POST /internal/psp/enrollments/noah/reliance``
(exact prefix depends on how :mod:`app.main` mounts this router).

Prefill business KYB data for Noah hosted onboarding:

Calls Noah ``POST /v1/onboarding/:CustomerID/prefill`` with ``Type: BusinessCustomerPrefill``.
Use this before ``POST /internal/psp/enrollments/noah/hosted-onboarding`` to ensure the hosted
flow is treated as Business KYB rather than defaulting to Individual.

Docs: https://docs.noah.com/api-reference/prefill-customer-details

post.summary: [Noah] Hosted onboarding: POST session + persist link (Standard model)
post.description:
Start Noah **hosted** onboarding for a ``CustomerID`` (path = ``noah_customer_id`` or
``subject_id``). Same ``subject_*`` / ``psp_customer_links`` rules as Reliance; typical
use is ``subject_type=organization`` when you have no KYB payload yet.

**Noah request body** (built by the service): ``Metadata``, ``ReturnURL``, ``FiatOptions``.

**Link status after success**

- Noah **201** — row set to ``approved`` (Noah: customer already onboarded for requested fiat).
- Noah **200** / **202** — row set to ``submitted`` (user must open ``HostedURL`` and/or wait
  for async review). Wire **customer webhooks** later to finalize ``approved`` / ``declined``.

**Errors:** **502** on Noah API failure; **503** if Noah not configured or signing PEM invalid.

PSPConfigurationAdminItem.title: PSPConfigurationAdminItem
PSPConfigurationAdminItem.description: One `psp_configurations` row for admin list APIs.

PSPConfigurationAdminPatch.title: PSPConfigurationAdminPatch
PSPConfigurationAdminPatch.description: Partial update for a single psp_configurations row (routing rules only).

PSPConfigurationReasonOnly.title: PSPConfigurationReasonOnly
PSPConfigurationReasonOnly.description: Optional note when toggling a configuration via admin API.

payment_rails_inserted.title: Payment Rails Inserted
payment_rails_inserted.description: New payment_rails rows created this call (0 if all rails already existed).

psp_configurations_inserted.title: Psp Configurations Inserted
psp_configurations_inserted.description: New psp_configurations rows created this call (0 if all defaults already existed for this environment).

message.title: Message
message.default: Idempotent: skipped rows that already existed for this environment.

PSPSeedDefaultsResponse.title: PSPSeedDefaultsResponse
PSPSeedDefaultsResponse.description: Result of idempotent default seed (CLI script uses the same logic).

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of users matching the filters

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of users returned (None if no limit)

PaginationMetadata.title: PaginationMetadata
PaginationMetadata.description: Pagination metadata for user transaction metrics.

PaymentRailAdminItem.title: PaymentRailAdminItem
PaymentRailAdminItem.description: One `payment_rails` row for admin list APIs.

PendingLinkDetails.title: PendingLinkDetails
PendingLinkDetails.description: Details of a pending KYC verification link.

PrivyToken.title: PrivyToken
PrivyToken.description: Schema for Privy BYOA JWT response.

ResetCodeSchema.title: ResetCodeSchema
ResetCodeSchema.description: Schema for requesting a password reset code.

reset_code.title: Reset Code
reset_code.description: The reset code sent to the user's email

psp_name.title: Psp Name
psp_name.description: PSP name (mpesa, cellulant, etc.) or null to use priority-based routing

SetPreferredPSPRequest.title: SetPreferredPSPRequest
SetPreferredPSPRequest.description: Request to set preferred PSP for a capability.

UpdatePasswordSchema.title: UpdatePasswordSchema
UpdatePasswordSchema.description: Schema for updating a user's password.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of orders for the user

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: Whether there are more orders available

UserOrdersResponse.title: UserOrdersResponse
UserOrdersResponse.description: Response schema for paginated user orders.

UserProfileResponse.title: UserProfileResponse
UserProfileResponse.description: Response schema for user profile information.

UserResponse.title: UserResponse
UserResponse.description: Schema for user response (without password hash).

totalVolume.title: Totalvolume
totalVolume.description: Sum of transaction amounts in the specified currency

totalRevenue.title: Totalrevenue
totalRevenue.description: Revenue after fees and costs (only from SETTLED/REFUNDED orders)

UserTransactionMetricsResponse.title: UserTransactionMetricsResponse
UserTransactionMetricsResponse.description: Response schema for user transaction metrics endpoint.

wallets.title: Wallets
wallets.description: List of unique wallet addresses used by the user

UserWalletsResponse.title: UserWalletsResponse
UserWalletsResponse.description: Response schema for user wallet addresses.

VerifyOtpResponse.title: VerifyOtpResponse
VerifyOtpResponse.description: Schema for OTP verify response (tokens + user).

VerifyOtpSchema.title: VerifyOtpSchema
VerifyOtpSchema.description: Schema for verifying OTP and getting JWT.

webhook_url.title: Webhook Url
webhook_url.description: New webhook URL for receiving API events. Set to null to remove.

webhook_secret.title: Webhook Secret
webhook_secret.description: New secret for webhook signature verification (8-128 characters). Set to null to remove.

WebhookConfigUpdateRequest.description: Request schema for updating webhook configuration.

key_id.title: Key Id
key_id.description: ID of the updated API key

has_webhook_secret.title: Has Webhook Secret
has_webhook_secret.description: Whether a webhook secret is now configured

WebhookConfigUpdateResponse.title: WebhookConfigUpdateResponse
WebhookConfigUpdateResponse.description: Response schema for webhook configuration updates.
